text:
Once upon a time in the heartland of a vast, green country, under a sky so clear you could hear the songbirds singing it away, there lived a young girl named Poppy. Poppy adored spring more than anything else. She woke up to the gentle tickling of wild roses brushing against her window panes.

One serene spring morning, as the first buds sprang open and the dawn sang a tender song, Poppy decided to organize a garden party. She gathered friends, older sister Lily, her curious cat Rufus, and her fluffy bunny neighbour, Flopsy. They spent the morning sketching plans, planting flowers, and filling buckets with water from a nearby stream.

Poppy showed her friends how to weave delicate spiderwebs around the burgeoning blooms. Lily crafted tiny flower crowns out of lacey cloth. Rufus found a cozy nook by the pond to read old mice tales, while Flopsy chanted a lullaby that lulled the squirrels into
